title: China-to-Saint Lucia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC — IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 Evidence for PV Inverters in Saint Lucia Utility Projects
rows.target_requirement: Saint Lucia's standards environment is influenced by UK / IEC legacy practice and by Eastern Caribbean / CARICOM regional procurement norms. For grid-tied solar PV inverters, EMC evidence should be prepared around IEC 62920 for PV power conversion equipment and applicable IEC 61000-series emissions and immunity tests, including harmonic current and voltage fluctuation considerations where required by LUCELEC or the project owner. Because tourism, public buildings, resorts, and island distribution feeders can be sensitive to power-quality issues, project submissions should clearly identify inverter model, firmware, power rating, conducted and radiated emissions evidence, harmonic limits, and any site-specific filtering or export-control configuration.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility emissions, immunity, harmonics, and voltage fluctuation standards as applicable
SLBS standards-adoption context — confirm applicable IEC or regional standards with Saint Lucia Bureau of Standards
LUCELEC project power-quality and interconnection conditions — confirm project-specific harmonic and EMC requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence commonly relies on GB/T 37408-2021 and related China domestic EMC tests performed by CNAS-accredited laboratories. The test methods may overlap conceptually with IEC 61000-series methods, but China-only GB/T reports do not automatically satisfy IEC 62920 or the exact IEC 61000 evidence expected by SLBS, LUCELEC, lenders, resorts, or public-sector project owners in Saint Lucia. Reports prepared at China 220/380 V settings should also be reviewed for relevance to Saint Lucia 230/400 V operation.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes China domestic EMC requirements
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T EMC reports are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62920 and IEC 61000 evidence in Saint Lucia. Exporters should provide IEC 62920 EMC type-test evidence for the exact inverter model, applicable IEC 61000 harmonic and immunity reports, and an engineering note confirming whether the reports cover the 230/400 V, 50 Hz configuration supplied to Saint Lucia. Where coastal installations use long AC cable runs, filters, export-limiting devices, or communications equipment, include the final configuration in the EMC file.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific but commonly mandatory for LUCELEC grid review, SLBS or owner standards compliance, and lender or public-sector procurement where IEC evidence is specified
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese GB/T EMC evidence is domestic conformity evidence and does not automatically transfer to Saint Lucia IEC-referenced project acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on China-only GB/T EMC evidence as the sole EMC file for Saint Lucia. Prepare IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 reports for the specific model and confirm that they cover the Saint Lucia 230/400 V, 50 Hz configuration. Verify current SLBS, LUCELEC, and project-owner EMC expectations before shipment.

rows.requirement: LUCELEC / NURC Grid-Connection Settings — 230/400 V, 50 Hz, IEC 62116 Anti-Islanding, and IEC 61727 Interface Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Saint Lucia's public electricity supply is operated by LUCELEC and regulated by the National Utility Regulatory Commission (NURC). Grid-tied solar PV inverters should be configured for the Saint Lucia 230/400 V, 50 Hz supply system and for LUCELEC project-specific protection settings. The frequency is the same 50 Hz frequency commonly used in China, but the voltage differs: Saint Lucia uses 230/400 V while China commonly uses 220/380 V under GB/T 156. For utility-interconnected PV inverters, project documentation should include IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence and IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, plus documented country-code or custom firmware settings for voltage, frequency, trip thresholds, reconnection delay, and export-control functions.
target_standards: IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
LUCELEC interconnection and project commissioning conditions — confirm project-specific voltage, protection, metering, and export-control settings
NURC electricity-sector regulation context for Saint Lucia utility projects
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408-2021, and domestic anti-islanding reports. These documents are usually prepared for China's 220/380 V, 50 Hz grid conditions and Chinese grid-operator protection settings. They do not automatically prove suitability for Saint Lucia's 230/400 V, 50 Hz LUCELEC network. Even where frequency is the same, voltage thresholds, reconnection timing, ride-through behaviour, neutral/earthing assumptions, and export-control settings must be reviewed for the Saint Lucia installation.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 156 — 标准电压 (Standard voltages; China 220/380 V, 50 Hz baseline)
rows.gap: Gap: China grid certificates do not establish LUCELEC acceptance. Exporters should obtain the LUCELEC project connection conditions, set the inverter profile for Saint Lucia 230/400 V at 50 Hz, document anti-islanding thresholds, voltage and frequency trip points, reconnection delay, ride-through limits, and export-control settings, and ensure IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence covers the supplied firmware profile. Do not state that Saint Lucia voltage matches China; only the 50 Hz frequency matches.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for any grid-tied solar PV installation accepted by LUCELEC or reviewed under NURC-regulated utility conditions; exact settings are project-specific gates
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China domestic grid-review needs but is not transferable to LUCELEC acceptance without Saint Lucia configuration review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Saint Lucia grid settings as 230/400 V at 50 Hz, not China 220/380 V. Reconfigure and document the inverter profile for LUCELEC project conditions and obtain IEC 62116 / IEC 61727 evidence covering that profile. Confirm current NURC and LUCELEC requirements before shipment or commissioning.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence, SLBS Standards Context, and Coastal Hurricane / Salt-Mist Derating
rows.target_requirement: Saint Lucia's standards body is the Saint Lucia Bureau of Standards (SLBS), while renewable-energy projects operate in an Eastern Caribbean OECS / CARICOM tourism economy with policy interest in geothermal and solar generation. PV inverter safety files should include IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 type-test evidence for the supplied model or model family. Because many installations are coastal, exposed to hurricane winds, salt mist, heat, humidity, and sea-freight logistics through Port Castries, the safety file should also document enclosure rating, corrosion protection, surge protection, mounting constraints, thermal derating, storage and transport conditions, spare-parts strategy, and English installation / maintenance manuals suitable for local installers.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
SLBS standards-adoption and conformity context — confirm applicable national adoptions and project requirements with Saint Lucia Bureau of Standards
Project-owner and insurer requirements for hurricane exposure, coastal salt-mist corrosion, surge protection, and thermal derating
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter safety documentation commonly includes GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, factory test records, and voluntary CQC or CGC certificates. These China domestic documents may support engineering review but are not automatic substitutes for IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates when a Saint Lucia project, lender, utility reviewer, insurer, or public procurement file requests IEC evidence. China domestic documentation may also omit Caribbean-specific environmental declarations for hurricane mounting, salt-mist corrosion, high humidity, surge exposure, English manuals, or Port Castries sea-freight handling.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CQC / CGC voluntary certification schemes for China PV inverter products
rows.gap: Gap: China domestic safety certificates do not automatically satisfy Saint Lucia IEC-referenced safety expectations. Exporters should provide IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates and reports for the exact model family, environmental ratings and derating curves for 230/400 V, 50 Hz operation in tropical coastal conditions, salt-mist and corrosion declarations where applicable, surge protection coordination, hurricane-rated mounting assumptions, English manuals, and packing / storage instructions for sea freight through Port Castries. Confirm whether SLBS, LUCELEC, NURC, the project owner, or insurer requires additional evidence before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific but commonly mandatory where IEC 62109 evidence, SLBS conformity review, LUCELEC interconnection, lender procurement, insurer review, or public-sector procurement is specified
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and voluntary CQC / CGC certificates are China domestic evidence and are not automatically accepted for Saint Lucia IEC-referenced project files

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] A Saint Lucia PV inverter safety file should lead with IEC 62109-1/-2 evidence and environmental documentation for coastal, hurricane-exposed service. China GB/T 37408-2021, NB/T 32004, and CQC / CGC certificates may be useful background, but they are not automatic substitutes for Saint Lucia IEC-referenced project evidence. Verify SLBS, LUCELEC, NURC, owner, and insurer expectations before shipment.
